methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Hexana is a seed-stage nuclear energy company that has raised €21M to date under founder Gabriel Lévy. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across the nuclear energy sector: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities, including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Standard peer-median methodology is not applicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private comparables, but filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage seed companies yields fewer than the three datapoints required for robust median analysis. Of the available nuclear peers, only one operates at an adjacent stage, while zero share both stage and valuation tier characteristics with Hexana. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the specificity of advanced nuclear development economics and capital requirements.

The analysis therefore cannot support a triangulated valuation range at this time. Material uncertainties preclude reliable benchmarking: the scarcity of seed-stage nuclear comparables, heterogeneity in technology maturity across the peer set, and the early-stage nature of Hexana's business model all limit inference from existing transactions. A valuation would require alternative methods—cost-of-capital approaches, venture capital method analysis, or expansion of the comparison set to include adjacent deep-tech infrastructure sectors—to establish defensible boundaries. Revisiting this analysis as the company progresses or as more seed/Series A nuclear transactions emerge would be prudent.
